title: polypodium vulgare f. cambricum
intro: Polypodium vulgare f. cambricum, also known as spleenwort, is a type of fern native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its kidney-shaped fronds and ability to grow on rocks and walls. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of spleenwort include reducing swelling and inflammation, treating wounds and burns, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of spleenwort can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and grinding the fronds to create a powder for use in topical creams and salves.
